title: NetToolsKit Extension Governance Catalog

classes.title: Agent lane
classes.loadingBoundary: Agents declare orchestration roles and defaults, but do not own provider projection or runtime transport policy.

classes.title: Skill lane
classes.loadingBoundary: Skills provide reusable specialist capability packs and must stay behind runtime routing and instruction governance.

classes.title: Hook lane
classes.loadingBoundary: Hooks define lifecycle entrypoints and must remain explicit, auditable, and runtime-bound.

classes.title: Prompt lane
classes.loadingBoundary: Prompts are consumer-facing assets and must not silently redefine canonical instructions or runtime policy.

classes.title: Runtime projection lane
classes.loadingBoundary: Projected runtime surfaces consume canonical definitions but do not become new authored sources of truth.

classes.title: Future plugin package lane
classes.loadingBoundary: Plugins remain a planned extension class and must not bypass runtime, MCP, instruction, or validation governance when introduced.

governanceRules: Canonical authored extension roots live under definitions/ before any provider projection or generated mirror.
Agents, skills, and hooks are repository-owned lanes; provider prompts and runtime projections are consumer lanes.
Runtime projections may consume canonical definitions but cannot redefine repository-owned policy.
Future plugins require an explicit manifest contract and validator coverage before they become a first-class runtime loading surface.
Extension loading must stay explicit and auditable; no hidden discovery roots are allowed.
